title: Anger Management - 211info

Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details ALLIES IN CHANGE ALLIES IN CHANGE BEAVERTON ABUSE INTERVENTION GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers individual, couples, and group counseling. Helps people with a wide variety of issues including those dealing with depression, stress, anxiety, building better connections, communication and successful relationships with others, dealing with significant changes and transitions in life related to relationships, work, family, health, and other issues. Also has a sub-specialty in domestic violence counseling for abusers and anger management. 1675 SW Marlow Avenue Suite 110 Portland, OR 97225 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-7pm, Friday 9am-5pm. Appointments available outside of office hours by request. Email (503) 297-7979 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE 2ND CHANCE SHELTER PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services such as limited assistance obtaining Oregon ID, mailing addresses, GED tutoring, and essential supplies as available (such as toothpaste, shampoo, soap, and deodorant). Also provides job search assistance such as preparing a resume, completing job applications, and interview techniques. Other services include: counseling including anger management, detox and rehab referrals, Oregon Health Plan enrollment assistance, and computer and internet access. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 967-8545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous), and classes (smoking cessation and anger management). 231 Lyon Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Support Groups: -Men's Narcotics Anonymous: Monday 7pm-8pm -Narcotics Anonymous: Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday/Sunday 7pm-8pm -Wellbriety: Wednesday 6pm-7pm -Gamblers Anonymous: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:30pm -Women for Sobriety: TBD Email (503) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE CHANCE CORVALLIS ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous). 456 SW Monroe Avenue Suite 108 Corvallis, OR 973335412 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies, call for current schedule Email (541) 224-2750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE CHANCE LEBANON ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous). 365 S Main Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday 9am-3:30pm Email (541) 248-2750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHRISTIANS AS FAMILY ADVOCATES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers Christian faith-based mental health services, including counseling for trauma, adjustment, anger, domestic violence, parenting, marriage, depression, and anxiety; individual and group therapy for children, teens, and adults; and filial, play, and neurofeedback therapy including EEG Cygnet and IASIS microcurrent transcranial. Safe and Sound In protocol (SSP) providers. Provides intervention services to batterers and support to survivors, as well as therapeutic services for children and youth who have been impacted by divorce and/or domestic violence. Offers supervised visitation and parenting classes. 921 Country Club Road Suite 222 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but cannot work with sex offender population Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 9am-6pm, Wednesday 9:15am-5pm (541) 686-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ENFOQUE DE LA FAMILIA COUNSELING CENTER COUNSELING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally-specific counseling with a focus on the Hispanic and Latino community for substance use, anger management, and/or domestic violence. Services are provided through group classes, family, individual, or marriage counseling, one-on-one problem-solving, and family activities. 205 SE 3rd Street Suite 200 Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Counseling: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Groups and classes: vary on weekday evenings (503) 681-4870 Get Directions More Details GOOD SAMARITAN MINISTRIES INTERNATIONAL HEADQUARTERS GSM BEAVERTON GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides faith-based group classes on a variety of topics, including anger management, men’s group, mutual support group, and body image and weight management. 7929 SW Cirrus Drive Beaverton, OR 97008 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by group or class Email (503) 644-2339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH TRIBES HEALTH SATELLITE CENTER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to services including but not limited to mental health assessments, counseling services, emotional/life skills coaching, and trauma recovery/trauma informed care. Also provides resources related to substance abuse such as assessment, counseling, anger management classes, relapse prevention, and aftercare. 6000 New Way Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Native American individuals of federally recognized tribes Hours: Phone Line: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Lobby: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 884-1841 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Community mental health services include individual, group, and family therapy for adults and children; help with parenting issues; anger management classes for adults and adolescents; psychiatric assessment and medication management; case management for people with severe and persistent mental illness; stress reduction classes; and pain management classes. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC PREVENTION AND RECOVERY TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides inpatient and outpatient substance use disorder treatment services, including assessment and individual and group treatment/education. If needed, services are coordinated with the local court system, SAFE Court, and Community Corrections to fulfill legal obligations. Other services include domestic violence intervention services, moral reconation groups, anger management, dual diagnosis treatment, and urinalysis and other screening tools. Substance use disorder prevention education available for children, parents, and community members. Also provides gambling assessment and treatment. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Assessment walk-in hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Office: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm, evening services available Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING HOPE CHURCH LIVING FREE MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Christian faith-based support groups and classes for individuals experiencing addiction to alcohol, drugs, food, or pornography, as well as for those dealing with anger, depression, grief, and divorce. Offers Beyond the Walls, a class for families with loved ones who are incarcerated. Child care is available for support groups and classes. Also provides a community meal. Worship services are offered but are not required for participation. 2711 NE Andresen Road Vancouver, WA 98661 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Community meal: Tuesday 5:30pm -Support groups: 7:20pm, support group times vary -Worship service: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:20pm Email (360) 944-3905 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST KLAMATH BASIN LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NW KLAMATH FALLS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers individual and family mental health and wellness counseling, anger management classes, parenting classes, medication management, alcohol and drug treatment, and prevention programs. 2545 N Eldorado Avenue Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5:30pm, Tuesday 1pm-5:30pm, Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 883-3471 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST YAMHILL COUNTY LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NW MCMINNVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual, family, and couples counseling. Offers programs to address behavioral challenges in children and adolescents. Additional services include psychiatric assessments, battered intervention programs, and anger management counseling. Parent education classes are offered throughout the year. 435 NE Evans Street Suite A McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, additional hours by appointment Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH AND SUD ADDICTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Outpatient program offering a broad range of mental health, substance abuse and addictions, and prevention services. Services include mental health and developmental disabilities services for adults, children and families, and older adults crisis intervention; group and marriage counseling; programs to reintroduce hospital patients into their communities; help those experiencing homelessness; providing a transition from opioid and opioid-based medications to medications used to limit and reduce withdrawal symptoms (Please note: not a methadone clinic.) Behavioral Health and Wellness programs can fulfill State requirements for Driving Under the Influence (DUI)/Minor in Possession (MIP) education and treatment. Also provides Domestic Violence Intervention Program (DVIP) groups, relapse prevention groups, family counseling, and parenting and anger management classes. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 519-7126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR YOUTH FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services, including counseling, support groups, violence and anger prevention training, life skills groups, parenting classes, new parenting groups, crime victims support, community information and referral and temporary housing. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Ages 6-21 and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE2ANOTHER ONE 2 ANOTHER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support services to bridge communication between families and professionals. Services may include attending meetings at work or school with individuals and families and/or attending court appearances to take notes and provide emotional support. Does not provide legal advice or aid. Other services include parenting classes, anger management classes, and children's activities. 1113 Hill Street Suite K Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies based on services Email (541) 570-0284 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON WASHINGTON HEALTH NETWORK HERMISTON CHANGE THROUGH COMPASSION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community-based supports that prevent, reduce, or interrupt violence and/or prevent involvement in the criminal legal system through anger management, parenting, and domestic violence intervention education and skills clases. 140 SW 11th Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Caring Dads: Male-identifying individuals All other services: Individuals returning from incarceration and/or currently under supervision, such as probation or parole. Hours: Varies by service (541) 663-4104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON WASHINGTON HEALTH NETWORK CHANGE THROUGH COMPASSION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community-based supports that prevent, reduce, or interrupt violence and/or prevent involvement in the criminal legal system through anger management, parenting, and domestic violence intervention education and skills clases. 200 SE Hailey Avenue Suite 307 Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Caring Dads: Male-identifying individuals All other services: Individuals returning from incarceration and/or currently under supervision, such as probation or parole. Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 663-4104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG ADULT SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND ADDICTIONS TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following substance use disorder services: -Outpatient treatment and education for adolescents and adults -Individual, family, and group counseling options -DUII Diversion Treatment including DMV Certification -Substance abuse assessments and assist with referrals to inpatient treatment facilities if deemed necessary -Comprehensive prevention program -Anger management courses -Services related to gambling difficulties 215 N G Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 947-6021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG CHRISTMAS VALLEY SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND ADDICTIONS TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following substance use disorder services: -Outpatient treatment and education for adolescents and adults -Individual, family, and group counseling options -DUII Diversion Treatment including DMV Certification -Substance abuse assessments and assist with referrals to inpatient treatment facilities if deemed necessary -Comprehensive prevention program -Anger management courses -Services related to gambling difficulties 87520 Bay Road Christmas Valley, OR 97641 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 947-6021 4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE JOURNEYS LLC TREATMENT AND COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides state-certified outpatient treatment for DUII and Substance Use Disorders (SUD), including relapse prevention and addictions counseling (problem gambling included). Also offers support for parenting skills, trauma, stress, anger management, life skills, and wellness coaching. Telehealth services available via audio or video conferencing. 18801 SW Martinazzi Avenue Suite 206 Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Age 21 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-8pm by appointment only Email (503) 989-0991 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TERAS COUNSELING INC ANGER MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual anger management sessions. Does not offer group sessions. Each person's anger management plan is individualized to their unique situation. The number of sessions may vary but a minimum of 12 sessions is recommended. 3945 SE Hawthorne Boulevard Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Individuals who have alleged, adjucated, pending, or other history of domestic violence are ineligible. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-9pm, Saturday by appointment only Email (503) 719-5250 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH CONTACT INC STUDENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides alcohol and drug prevention services that include substance abuse counseling, anger management, and family counseling. 447 SE Baseline Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Students age 19 and younger who are enrolled in a participating Washington County school Hours: Monday-Friday, hours vary by service Email (503) 640-4222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALLIES IN CHANGE ABUSE INTERVENTION GROUPS ALLIES IN CHANGE ABUSE INTERVENTION GROUPS ALLIES IN CHANGE ABUSE INTERVENTION GROUPS ALLIES IN CHANGE ABUSE INTERVENTION GROUPS ALLIES IN CHANGE ABUSE INTERVENTION GROUPS CHANCE PEER SUPPORT SERVICES CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHRISTIANS AS FAMILY ADVOCATES CHRISTIANS AS FAMILY ADVOCATES ENFOQUE DE LA FAMILIA COUNSELING CENTER COUNSELING CENTER GOOD SAMARITAN MINISTRIES INTERNATIONAL HEADQUARTERS GSM BEAVERTON GROUPS AND CLASSES KLAMATH TRIBES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CLINIC LIFEWAYS INC MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NW KLAMATH FALLS LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NW MCMINNVILLE NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH AND SUD ADDICTIONS NEXT DOOR YOUTH FAMILY SERVICES ONE2ANOTHER ONE 2 ANOTHER SUPPORT SERVICES OREGON WASHINGTON HEALTH NETWORK CHANGE THROUGH COMPASSION OREGON WASHINGTON HEALTH NETWORK CHANGE THROUGH COMPASSION OUTBACK STRONG SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND ADDICTIONS TREATMENT OUTBACK STRONG SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND ADDICTIONS TREATMENT SAFE JOURNEYS LLC TREATMENT AND COUNSELING TERAS COUNSELING INC ANGER MANAGEMENT YOUTH CONTACT INC STUDENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
